title: Sealed Classes
description: Represent restricted hierarchies with ultimate safety.

slides.title: Enums on Steroids 💊
slides.content:
## Advanced States

Enums (`BASIC`, `PREMIUM`) are great for simple constants. But what if each specific state needs to hold unique customized data?

For example, an API fetch can be exactly three things: `Loading`, `Success` (holding the downloaded data), or `Error` (holding the specific error message).

slides.title: The Sealed Class 🛡️
slides.content:
A **Sealed Class** securely restricts a class hierarchy to a strictly known, absolutely limited set of subclasses.

Like an Enum, the compiler definitively tracks every structurally possible option dynamically.

quizData.question: If an Enum securely limits identical static options, what radically does a Sealed Class inherently add?

options.text: It allows essentially each explicitly restricted structural option securely to actively hold completely different custom data intelligently.

quizData.explanation: If it crashes, `.Error` specifically carries the unique string message natively. The `.Loading` state fundamentally requires completely zero underlying data dynamically.

slides.title: Seamless When 🔥
slides.content: Because the compiler guarantees these are absolutely the ONLY three subclasses natively possible globally, utilizing `when` elegantly unlocks the data payload flawlessly intelligently completely safely natively unconditionally.
